front: Figure: first chancellor of the unified German Empire, who pursued the pragmatic foreign-policy philosophy of realpolitik

Figure: began contributing to the anti-revolutionary newspaper Kreuz-Zeitung after entering the conservative religious social circle of the von Gerlach brothers while managing his father Alexander's estate
work: Early Life & Rise in Prussian Politics

front: Figure: selected by Frederick William IV to represent Prussia at the Diet of Frankfurt
work: Early Life & Rise in Prussian Politics

front: Event: 1862 speech in which he declared the great issues of the day would be decided not by speeches and resolutions but by "blood and iron"
back: Blood and Iron Speech (Otto von Bismarck)
work: Blood and Iron & the Unification of Germany

front: Figure: as Prussian chancellor, led the process of German unification
work: Blood and Iron & the Unification of Germany

front: Event: his editing of this telegram, altered to make it look as though King Wilhelm had insulted the French ambassador, helped provoke the Franco-Prussian War
work: The Franco-Prussian War & the Ems Dispatch

front: Figure: the Ems Dispatch, a message from the Prussian king, was sent to this statesman before he released an edited version to incite the French public toward war
work: The Franco-Prussian War & the Ems Dispatch

front: Battle: his victory here led to the new German Empire's annexation of Alsace
work: The Franco-Prussian War & the Ems Dispatch

front: Figure: enacted the Kulturkampf, an anti-Catholic campaign, while declaring political questions would be settled with iron and blood
Law: set of Kulturkampf measures implemented by Bismarck, for which Rudolf Virchow coined the term "Kulturkampf"

Legend: in this apocryphal story, the biologist Rudolf Virchow challenged Bismarck to a duel in which each would eat a sausage, one infected with deadly bacteria or parasites, and Bismarck reportedly declined
back: The Great Sausage Duel (Otto von Bismarck and Rudolf Virchow)
work: The Great Sausage Duel with Rudolf Virchow

front: Figure: Ferdinand Lassalle unsuccessfully lobbied this leader for government support of his General German Workers' Association (ADAV) before Lassalle's death
Law: banned the SPD after two assassination attempts against Wilhelm I; lapsed as soon as their author was forced from office
Figure: introduced basic health insurance, accident insurance, and social security as part of a plan to undermine the SPD's popularity
Event: conference at which Russia and Austria gained no territory, convened by Bismarck to organize European spheres of influence during the Scramble for Africa
Work: John Tenniel's Punch cartoon depicting Bismarck's 1890 dismissal by Kaiser Wilhelm II as a ship's captain being ordered off his own boat
